ticker: Coverage across 847 sources 24 hours a day

major_stories.title: Federal Judiciary Appointments Reshape American Law for Decades
major_stories.summary: 247 lifetime federal judicial appointments have shifted the ideological balance of 9 circuit courts. Each confirmation generates modest coverage but the cumulative effect will shape American law for 30-40 years.

timeline.event: 87 confirmations — highest single year total in modern history

major_stories.analysis: This is the most consequential long-term political story receiving the least aggregated coverage. Each confirmation hearing gets modest press but 247 lifetime appointments will determine the outcome of cases involving healthcare, voting rights, and civil liberties for the next 40 years.

major_stories.title: Voting Infrastructure Under Sustained Legislative Pressure Across 28 States
major_stories.summary: Since 2021 over 28 states have enacted restrictions on mail-in ballot access. Each bill is reported in isolation. The cumulative picture is never assembled by mainstream media.

timeline.event: Georgia SB 441 restricts drop box locations to county offices only
Federal appeals court upholds Arizona signature verification rules
14 additional states pass absentee ballot restrictions

major_stories.analysis: Drop box counts have been reduced by 41% on average in affected counties. Absentee ballot request windows shortened by 18 days on average. Each individual bill received 1.2 days of media coverage on average before the next story took over.

major_stories.title: Immigration Court Backlog Reaches Record 3.7 Million Pending Cases
major_stories.summary: Every week brings new executive actions and political rhetoric on immigration. The operational reality — a court system at total collapse — is rarely the lead story.

timeline.event: 14th immigration executive order issued in current administration
Record 3.7M pending cases confirmed — EOIR funding request denied
Deportation numbers confirmed 18% below stated enforcement targets

major_stories.analysis: Political rhetoric and actual enforcement are inversely correlated. Average immigration case wait time is now 4.3 years. Media coverage focused 94% on announcements and 6% on actual outcomes and data.

fading_stories.title: Special Counsel Investigation Into Campaign Finance
fading_stories.description: Launched with major press attention 14 months ago. No charges filed, no public updates from DOJ. Coverage dropped 94% after week 3.

fading_stories.title: Congressional Oversight Hearings on AI in Government
fading_stories.description: Report due to Congress was promised for Q3 2025. Now 6 months overdue. No hearing scheduled. Generated 3 days of coverage then vanished completely.

outcomes.title: 34 State AGs File Joint Suit Against EPA Rollback Rules
outcomes.description: After 41 environmental rules were reversed with minimal press coverage, state attorneys general coordinated a multistate legal challenge citing APA violations.
outcomes.result: Case filed Feb 2026 in D.C. Circuit. Preliminary injunction granted on 3 of 41 rules. Trial date set for Oct 2026.

narratives.title: Voter fraud is widespread in mail-in ballots
narratives.description: Claim cited in 28 state bills. Documented fraud rate is 0.00004% of ballots cast per Stanford and MIT 2024 study. Narrative spread has increased 340% since 2020 with no change in underlying evidence.

narratives.title: Strict enforcement has significantly reduced border crossings
narratives.description: Claim peaked in late 2025 coverage. CBP data shows encounters declined but are attributable to seasonal patterns consistent with pre-2021 trends. Deportation numbers remain 18% below stated targets.
